headline: Cursor's $2B Run & Claude Code's Massive March Update Reshaping the Agentic Summer

stories.title: Cursor Hits $2B ARR: Cloud Agents Enter Era 3 of AI Coding
stories.summary: Cursor crossed $2B annual revenue milestone & launched cloud agents that run autonomously in sandboxes—literally flipping devs from writing code to managing agent factories. First-third of their PRs are now agent-generated.
stories.takeaway: Cursor's automations + cloud agents mark the shift from "prompt & monitor" to "set it & forget it"—devs are becoming engineering managers, not coders.

stories.title: Claude Code Gets Voice Mode, /loop Commands & 1M Token Context in March Blitz
stories.summary: Anthropic dropped voice mode, recurring task loops, 1M context window for Opus 4.6, MCP elicitation, and like 50+ micro-fixes—literally the most feature-packed month Claude Code has ever had.
stories.takeaway: Claude Code's March update makes it the first truly conversational + executable coding agent; the quota doubling is lowkey Anthropic testing how much users will abuse it.

stories.title: GitHub Copilot SDK Brings Agentic Execution Inside Your Apps
stories.summary: GitHub dropped a programmable SDK that lets devs embed the same Copilot execution layer directly into production apps—shifting from "AI as text" to "AI as infrastructure" that actually does stuff.
stories.takeaway: This is the play: instead of prompt→response, you define intent & constraints, agents plan/execute/iterate inside your product. The orchestration layer finally got commoditized.

stories.title: Cursor ACP + JetBrains IDEs: Full-Stack Agents Now Live in Your IDE
stories.summary: Cursor went multi-IDE through the Agent Client Protocol—now you get Cursor's agentic workflow inside IntelliJ, PyCharm, WebStorm while keeping JetBrains' native code intelligence. 30+ new MCP plugins shipped same day.
stories.takeaway: The IDE wars are officially over; agents are portable now. Cursor's not trying to replace JetBrains—it's the agentic layer on top of everything.

watchNext.title: Vibe Coding vs Vibe Engineering: The Terminology Debate Simon Willison Started
watchNext.why: Lowkey the philosophical underpinning of everything shipping this week—Willison's drawing a hard line between "vibes only" (irresponsible) and "agentic engineering" (intentional + tested). Matters for how you think about what these tools actually let you build.

watchNext.title: Replit Agent 3 vs Bolt vs v0: Full-Stack Vibe Coding Showdown
watchNext.why: The non-developer wave is real—Replit's agent hit 200+ minutes of autonomous building, Bolt's blazingly fast for prototypes, v0's still best for components. This week's benchmarks show who actually ships working stuff vs vibes.
